refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral, specifying 'compatible with safe remote operation' without prescribing how latency is achieved. The FR does not contain multiple obligations requiring splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) is less than or equal to 150 ms, ensuring compliance with safety requirements.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive latency monitoring and dynamic adjustment mechanisms
Separate control commands and feedback data streams to minimize processing delays
Utilize a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and data processing modules
Shared resources causing delays in data handling
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on system architecture to minimize latency, which can be implemented independently of specific communication protocols, supporting decoupling and modularity.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Latency measurement and monitoring are accurate and timely
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining separate DPs for communication protocol optimization and adaptive latency management to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating data flows and processing logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time data streams received and sent for processing and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange with external systems and components.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as NGHE Solution is designed to handle core data processing; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is more about physical control and less about data processing; would require significant diffusion.
Teleoperation involves remote control tasks but depends on processed data; partial containment but some diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not core data processing; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of real-time data processing with minimal buffering, as it centralizes data handling and processing logic, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some coupling.
Real-time constraints may require careful interface design to avoid latency issues across module boundaries.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing stages to better align with module boundaries, improving modularity without becoming implementation-specific.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving low end-to-end communication latency required for safe teleoperation.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some coupling, but diffusion scores are low for NGHE Solution (2), indicating minimal diffusion risk within the primary module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single implementation module, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) using telemetry and network link interfaces defined at the NGHE Solution module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, as this module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to avoid increased coupling that could impact latency.

If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing stages to better align with module boundaries, improving modularity without becoming implementation-specific.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module shows high containment and low diffusion risk, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary decomposition that could introduce complexity or risk. Monitoring interface complexity is recommended, but no immediate need for further sub-FR derivation exists.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR for low latency teleoperation.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for this functionality.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may increase coupling and impact latency.
evidence_needed: Telemetry and network link interface measurements confirming end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms).
Ongoing monitoring data of interface complexity and coupling metrics.
